tree.name: System Architecture
tree.description: CodeMerger is a highly decoupled desktop application leveraging a Python backend and a Vue 3 frontend to orchestrate context for Language Models. The architecture is cleanly divided into Frontend UI, Backend Engine, and Infrastructure components, all communicating via a PyWebView API bridge.

children.name: Frontend Application
children.description: The client-side Vue 3 application responsible for state management, interactive diff reviews, and communicating with the Python host.

children.name: Core Application Setup
children.description: Bootstrapping, routing, and primary view layouts for the Vue application.

files.description: The global root component that manages the application lifecycle. It intercepts global shortcut keys (like Ctrl+V) to bypass browser restrictions and routes them to the Python bridge.
The primary dashboard layout containing the project header, navigation, and core actions. Acts as the anchor for all full-screen modals.
An always-on-top, minimalist widget view designed to sit over an IDE. It implements custom logical-to-physical window drag calculations to ensure smooth movement on high-DPI screens.
The Vue 3 application entry point. Registers the router and custom directives before mounting to the DOM.
Vue Router configuration. Uses hash-based history to maintain compatibility with PyWebView's local file protocol rendering.

children.name: Global State Management
children.description: Reactive state composables that act as a decentralized store, replacing Vuex/Pinia.

children.name: Base Composables
children.description: Foundational state and system-level interactions.

files.description: The core reactive store holding the active project, file selections, and visualizer maps. Kept intentionally flat to ensure reactive properties can be easily tracked across the app.
Manages a stack-based tracking system for the application's hover-help feature. Uses spread operators to bypass Vue's array mutation reactivity limits across view boundaries.
Provides methods for system-level actions like resizing the window, loading native icons, and opening file explorers. Connects directly to the PyWebView bridge.
Handles project loading, profiling, and metadata synchronization. Updates the global state when the Python backend signals a project change.
An aggregator composable that sets up global event listeners for Python-dispatched events. Initializes the application and merges all modular composables into a single importable entity.
A standardized hook for registering and unregistering Escape key listeners. Used by modals to ensure they close cleanly and return focus.

children.name: Feature Composables
children.description: State management tied to specific complex application features.

files.description: Coordinates the flow of pasting AI responses and tracking pending changes. Communicates with the backend to verify clipboard contents and calculate diffs.
Encapsulates the complex multi-step state of the Project Starter. Manages the drafting, locking, and synchronization of markdown segments for the boilerplate generator.
Provides logic to seamlessly switch between rendered Markdown and raw textarea editing. It calculates scroll ratios to ensure the viewport remains stable during the transition.
Separates the complex feedback timings, transient alerts, and dynamic title truncation logic out of the CompactView component to keep it declarative.

children.name: UI Utilities & Directives
children.description: Helper functions, text dictionaries, and custom Vue directives.

children.name: Data Utilities
children.description: Stateless transform functions.

files.description: Calculates relative luminance for user-selected project hex colors. Determines whether light or dark text should be rendered over dynamic swatches.
Algorithms for the Architecture Explorer. Calculates dynamic treemap layouts based on file weights and recursively parses LLM JSON outputs into a nested tree.
The central aggregator for all Info Mode hover text dictionaries. Combines modular help files into a single lookup object.

children.name: Info Definitions
children.description: Text content for the hover-help system, split by domain.

files.description: Hover documentation for the primary dashboard buttons and profile management UI.
Hover documentation detailing the merge order list, filters, and tree selection logic.
Help text explaining the AI diff viewer, acceptance tracking, and comment parsing.
Detailed explanations of global application settings, token limits, and filetype indexing.
Documentation for the multi-step boilerplate workflow and prompt generation mechanics.
Help text for the Architecture Explorer's interactive map and contextual copying features.

children.name: Global Directives
children.description: App-wide Vue directives and CSS.

files.description: The root Tailwind CSS import file. Contains custom scrollbar styling, keyframe animations for click pulses, and prose overrides.
A custom Vue directive (`v-info`) that pushes and pops documentation keys to the global state stack on mouseenter/mouseleave events.

children.name: Dashboard Components
children.description: Widgets and layouts visible on the primary application screen.

children.name: Dashboard Layout
children.description: The structural elements of the main view.

files.description: The top bar containing the project title editor and masked logo renderer. Includes an alert icon synchronized with the background file monitor.
The secondary navigation row. Provides access to profile switching, the Visualizer, the Project Starter, and the File Manager.
The central action card for the workflow loop. Contains the primary 'Copy' and 'Paste' buttons, along with context limit warnings.
A persistent footer panel that subscribes to the global `infoMode` stack to display contextual documentation based on the user's cursor position.
A custom-built HSL color wheel implemented via a `<canvas>` element. Generates a pixel-perfect hue ring to overcome browser limitations.

children.name: Dashboard Utilities
children.description: Shared view components used across multiple dashboards.

files.description: A wrapper around `markdown-it` that intercepts rendering to style custom task-list checkboxes and apply CodeMerger's typography rules.
Renders line-by-line file comparisons. Delegates actual diff calculations and Pygments syntax highlighting to the Python backend for performance.
A recursive tree node component. Applies complex styling logic to indicate new files, filtered files, and directories that are 'visually complete'.
An overlay for defining custom prompt headers and footers. Interacts directly with the active project's config profile.
Displays the recently opened projects list. Filters paths dynamically and connects to the OS-native folder browser via Python.

children.name: Compact Widgets
children.description: Components specific to the minimized Compact View.

files.description: A transient animation layer that slides in to provide visual feedback (e.g., 'COPIED', 'PASTED') over the tiny widget interface.
The default 2-row layout for Compact Mode. Provides clearly visible copy/paste actions alongside new-file alerts.
An extreme space-saving layout that condenses all CodeMerger actions into a single narrow row for minimal screen intrusion.

children.name: File Management & Review
children.description: Complex modals for selecting context and verifying AI modifications.

children.name: File Manager Modals
children.description: Interfaces for selecting and ordering the codebase context.

files.description: The root orchestrator for the dual-panel file manager. Coordinates search filters and handles 'Add All' logic respecting safety thresholds.
Renders the hierarchical file tree. Implements shift-click range selection and syncs with the Python background scanner.
Manages the explicit merge order using drag-and-drop. Automatically colors token counts based on thresholds to warn against context bloat.
A small dialog for creating named configuration profiles. Includes toggles to inherit current file selections and prompt instructions.
An alert modal that appears on boot to notify the user if new default extensions (e.g., `.rs`, `.kt`) were automatically added during an update.

children.name: Review Components
children.description: Interfaces for auditing AI-generated code.

files.description: The primary response review container. Sorts the AI's segmented Markdown output into interactive tabs for Changes, Answers, and Verification.
An expandable accordion that renders any technical explanation or implementation plan the AI provided before outputting code.
Represents a single modified file in the review list. Provides Accept/Discard/Undo buttons and embeds the DiffViewer component.
The main interactive list grouping AI proposals into Modify, Create, and Delete sections. Handles bulk actions like 'Expand All'.
A dedicated view for testing instructions. Archives previous verification steps from the current session so users don't lose testing context.

children.name: Error Handlers
children.description: Specialized alerts for malformed LLM outputs.

files.description: Appears when an AI fails to return a valid JSON array for a file reorder request. Provides a one-click correction prompt for the user to paste back.
Appears when an AI fails to wrap files in the mandatory syntax markers. Halts execution to prevent file corruption and offers a correction prompt.

children.name: Advanced Features
children.description: Configuration, scaffolding workflows, and architectural visualization.

children.name: Settings UI & Core
children.description: The main settings application window.

files.description: The root settings container. Manages the sidebar navigation and holds a mutable clone of the global config until 'Save' is clicked.
Exposes toggles for file monitoring intervals, background secret scanning, and automated daily update checks.
Configuration for the tokenizer. Allows users to set maximum token thresholds to change UI warning colors.
Allows the user to explicitly define the path to a custom code editor executable for handling double-click file opening.

children.name: Settings Configuration
children.description: Data and prompt preference tabs.

files.description: A table interface for managing indexed file extensions. Allows users to add custom extensions or disable built-in ones to speed up scanning.
Provides text areas for modifying the global default headers and footers that are applied to newly created project profiles.
Configures the default parent directory where the Project Starter tool will attempt to scaffold new application folders.

children.name: Starter Modals 1
children.description: The initial data gathering phases for the Project Starter.

files.description: The root wizard for the Project Starter. Manages the persistent session state and tracks the maximum accessible step based on completion.
The first step in the wizard. Gathers the project name and allows linking an existing 'base project' folder for context.
An optional step that surfaces a mini file manager. Lets the user select specific reference files from a base project to pass to the LLM.
The Concept generation step. Bridges the user's initial goal with the LLM to output a structured markdown document detailing features and flows.
The Tech Stack step. Parses the AI's JSON recommendations into an interactive list detailing the architectural rationale for each language.
The Implementation Plan step. Feeds the Concept and Stack back to the LLM to generate a phase-by-phase development task list.

children.name: Starter Modals 2
children.description: Review, rewrite, and generation phases for the Project Starter.

files.description: The final step. Bundles all approved drafts into a master prompt to scaffold the project structure, `README.md`, and batch scripts to disk.
A completion screen confirming successful creation. Provides shortcuts to immediately load the new project into CodeMerger.
A widget used to review merged Markdown documents. Provides inline editing, rendering, and context-aware LLM feedback questions.
A specialized interface that allows users to review and lock specific sections of a draft (e.g., just the Database phase) before rewriting.
A side-panel that displays pre-written 'Guiding Questions'. Clicking one automatically builds a contextual prompt to challenge the LLM.
A draggable popup that allows the user to instruct the AI to modify the current drafts while explicitly respecting 'Locked' segments.

children.name: Starter Utilities
children.description: Helper components for the boilerplate workflow.

files.description: A simple dialogue that displays any explanatory notes the AI provided when returning a requested Rewrite of a starter segment.

children.name: Visualizer Components
children.description: The interactive Architecture Explorer interfaces.

files.description: The root container for the Architecture Explorer. Manages the visual state (Init, View, Update) and holds the parsed JSON map in memory.
The onboarding screen. Handles copying the JSON schema prompt and gracefully catching/admonishing the LLM if the returned tree omits files.
The primary interactive treemap. Dynamically sizes CSS boxes based on the file weight of each architectural domain using recursive calculation.
A list view displayed when diving into the deepest nodes of the map. Shows files alongside their AI-generated architectural logic descriptions.
A contextual sidebar. As the user navigates or hovers over nodes, this panel updates to show the specific responsibilities of that software layer.

children.name: Backend Engine
children.description: The Python-based core responsible for OS interactions, file system operations, text processing, and bridging with the Vue frontend.

children.name: Entry & API Endpoints
children.description: Startup routines and PyWebView API bridges.

children.name: Entry Points
children.description: Initialization and primary application state.

files.description: The main Python executable script. It handles Windows DPI-awareness bootstrapping, sets up logging, and initializes the PyWebView instance.
Maintains persistent global application state. It acts as the central memory hub for multi-instance syncing, saving window geometry, and tracking recent projects.
The final `Api` class exposed to the frontend. It inherits all segmented API part modules into a single object passed to PyWebView.
A shared base class for all API mixins. Provides common utilities like triggering native confirmation dialogs centered over the active monitor.
API methods specifically for window orchestration. Handles minimizing, restoring, and executing DPI-aware resize/move commands via JS triggers.

children.name: Core Endpoints
children.description: Standard API operations for configuration and merging.

files.description: Exposes getters and setters for the global `config.json`. Handles updating the active filetypes and managing default prompts.
Handles project lifecycle events like loading, renaming, and deleting profiles. Triggers native OS file dialogs for directory selection.
Interfaces with the file system scanners. Serves the hierarchical tree structure to the frontend and dynamically calculates token counts on demand.
Manages direct interactions with the OS clipboard using `pyperclip`, bypassing browser security popups. Triggers background secret scanning.
Routes pasted AI text to the Markdown parser and Diff generator. Applies finalized patches directly to disk.

children.name: Project Starter Endpoints
children.description: API methods dedicated to the boilerplate wizard.

files.description: Manages reading and writing the temporary Project Starter session state to disk, allowing users to safely close and resume the wizard.
Extracts XML-tagged segments from raw LLM output strings and maps them securely to internal state keys for the Vue interface.
Generates complex prompts dynamically by injecting user state, base project code, and specific template instructions based on the active step.
Executes the final generation phase. Safely creates target directories and writes the parsed code blocks, `README`, and scripts to the file system.
The aggregator class that merges all Project Starter API mixins. Also handles disabling standard app features while the wizard is active.

children.name: Project Logic
children.description: Managers for `.codemerger` configuration data.

children.name: Configuration IO
children.description: Reading, writing, and migrating project profiles.

files.description: A centralized repository of application constants, including UI timing variables, regex markers, API endpoints, and system paths.
Safely resolves system paths, abstracting whether the app is running as a bare Python script or a frozen PyInstaller executable.
Provides low-level atomic write operations. Employs a robust temp-file replacement pattern to prevent config corruption if the app crashes.
Handles backward compatibility by porting legacy `.allcode` flat files into the modern modular `.codemerger` profile structure.
The primary model representing a loaded project. Breaks profile data into chunks (selections, ui states, instructions) to optimize load times.
A centralized manager handling thread-safe active project assignment. Caches the raw disk inventory to prevent redundant directory walks.

children.name: File System
children.description: Disk scanning, text diffing, and content formatting.

children.name: Scanners & Builders
children.description: Directory traversal and data structures.

files.description: High-performance OS file walker. Enriches raw paths with `.gitignore` and extension metadata in a background pass to offload UI work.
Consumes the flat inventory from the scanner and constructs a nested Trie data structure. Injects visual styling flags (e.g., 'purple' hidden files) for the UI.
Uses YELP's `detect-secrets` library to perform heuristic checks on selected files, warning the user before copying sensitive keys to an LLM.
A background daemon that periodically checks the disk for newly created files. Communicates updates proactively to the Vue frontend via events.

children.name: AI Context Processing
children.description: Parsing, Diffing, and Re-assembling code strings.

files.description: Shared utility functions including `tiktoken` context counting, SHA-1 file hashing, and complex `.gitignore` pattern evaluation logic.
A static repository of complex, multi-line instruction strings used to command the LLM. Abstracts text out of the logic layer.
Loops through the active file selection and concatenates contents into a single string, applying mandatory Markdown wrappers and tags.
A 'Fuzzy' patch engine. Uses cascading strategies to surgically apply `ORIGINAL`/`UPDATED` diff blocks even if the LLM skewed the formatting.
Parses the raw Markdown from the clipboard. Slices out XML sections and executes the final write/delete instructions to the local disk.
Utilizes the `Pygments` library to generate static syntax-highlighted HTML tables. Replaces client-side highlighting to massively reduce frontend load.

children.name: Windows Platform
children.description: Native OS integration and orchestration.

children.name: Window Types & Handlers
children.description: UI window states and Win32 geometry math.

files.description: Reads and writes to the Windows Registry (HKCU/HKLM) to persist specific settings that dictate system-wide behaviors.
Calculates physical/logical pixel conversions. Relies on the `ctypes` Windows API to track monitor IDs and scaling factors during drags.
Generates a transient, frameless initialization window injected with a Base64 animated payload to display during boot.
Instantiates the primary PyWebView instance. Binds system-level events (minimize/restore) back into internal class methods.
Instantiates the Compact Mode widget. Employs a specific Win32 API hack (`WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW`) to hide the widget from the Windows Taskbar.
The central UI orchestrator. Tracks coordinates, manages transitions between the Main and Compact states, and handles application teardown.

children.name: Project Infrastructure
children.description: Configuration artifacts, CI/CD pipelines, build scripts, and standalone templates.

children.name: Root Configurations
children.description: Base level setup for the environment.

children.name: Frontend Setup
children.description: Node/Vue environment configurations.

files.description: Defines Node.js dependencies (Vue, Tailwind, Lucide, Markdown-it) and custom NPM scripts for concurrent local development.
Configuration for the Vite bundler. Instructs Vite to compile relative assets suitable for a `file://` based PyWebView host.
Tailwind definitions including custom theme colors (like `cm-blue`) and explicit paths for the PurgeCSS scanner.
Enables the PostCSS pipeline required by Tailwind to inject utility classes and auto-prefixer logic.
The base HTML skeleton. Mounts the Vue application div and loads the `main.js` module entry point.

children.name: Application Environment
children.description: Python, Executable, and GitHub configurations.

files.description: A pinned list of Python pip dependencies required by the backend, including `pywebview`, `detect-secrets`, and `tiktoken`.
The master list of known file extensions mapped to descriptions. Used to initially populate a user's indexing rules.
The PyInstaller configuration. Details the inclusion of raw data assets and Tcl/Tk bindings to compile the app into a single `.exe`.
The Inno Setup script. Defines the Windows installer behavior, including registry changes, context menu additions, and version migrations.
The CI/CD pipeline definition. Automatically triggers on version tags to build the frontend, freeze the executable, and draft a GitHub Release.

children.name: Executable Scripts
children.description: Standalone entry files and system processes.

files.description: A robust logging configuration that catches and swallows output stream errors when running in a headless windowed environment.
Queries the GitHub API to check for newer tags. If an update is approved, it downloads the installer and triggers the external GUI.
A small, self-contained Tkinter application. Compiled separately to wait for the main CodeMerger process to die before launching the new installer.
The master orchestrator batch script for development. Handles spinning up virtual environments, concurrent Dev servers, and executing PyInstaller.

children.name: Starter Assets
children.description: Raw text files and references used by the Project Starter tool.

children.name: Reference Guides
children.description: Templates and Q&A JSONs for prompting.

files.description: A JSON map providing 'Guiding Questions' specific to each segment of the Concept document for contextual LLM challenge prompts.
A JSON map providing 'Guiding Questions' specific to phases of the TODO Plan, ensuring the generated plan covers environment setup and edge cases.
The base Markdown template injected into the LLM context to ensure it generates a structurally sound and well-defined Concept outline.
The base Markdown template defining standard phase breakdowns (Setup -> UI -> Logic -> Deploy) for the LLM to follow when drafting tasks.
A generic README template that is injected and customized dynamically by the LLM during the final scaffolding phase.
A standard set of rules and directives intended to be kept inside the final project to instruct future LLMs on how to write code for the repo.

children.name: Boilerplate Scripts
children.description: Automation templates copied to new projects.

files.description: A simple INI-style version tracker file included as an example in bootstrapped repositories.
A reusable Windows batch script template that handles Git branch validation and tag pushing, given to newly scaffolded projects.
A boilerplate task runner for Python stacks. Automates creating a virtual environment using `uv` and installing dependencies.
A boilerplate task runner for Node.js stacks. Automates standard `npm` tasks like `run dev` and `run build` behind a unified command.
A boilerplate task runner for Docker stacks. Acts as a wrapper around `docker-compose` to handle builds, starting services, and shell access.

children.name: Documentation
children.description: Root level informational markdown files.

files.description: The primary public-facing documentation for the repository. Details the core workflow, Project Starter features, and development setup.
CodeMerger's self-hosting LLM rule file. Outlines architectural quirks (like DPI math) and specific constraints to aid language models in maintaining the code.
